topic: Curated factual technical knowledge compiled by the community

entries.q: What is the purpose of the Book of Secret Knowledge repository?
entries.a: The Book of Secret Knowledge repository is a collection of various materials and tools used daily in work, containing useful information gathered in one piece.

entries.q: Who is the Book of Secret Knowledge repository aimed at?
entries.a: The repository is aimed at System and Network administrators, DevOps, Pentesters, and Security Researchers.

entries.q: What type of license does the Book of Secret Knowledge repository use?
entries.a: The repository uses the MIT License.

entries.q: How can contributors participate in the Book of Secret Knowledge repository?
entries.a: Contributors can participate by making pull requests and adding valid and well-reasoned explanations about their changes or comments.

entries.q: What is the main purpose of the CLI Tools section in the Book of Secret Knowledge?
entries.a: The CLI Tools section provides a collection of command-line tools, including shells, shell plugins, and managers.

entries.q: What are some examples of shells listed in the Book of Secret Knowledge?
entries.a: Examples of shells listed include GNU Bash, Zsh, and tclsh.

entries.q: What is the purpose of the Table of Contents in the Book of Secret Knowledge?
entries.a: The Table of Contents provides a list of main chapters, including CLI Tools, GUI Tools, Web Tools, and more.

entries.q: How can users stay updated with changes to the Book of Secret Knowledge repository?
entries.a: Users can stay updated by subscribing to the GitHub RSS/Atom feed of commits.
